notes: Official Origin product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
You can definitely earn rewards on your rent payments without losing money to processing fees, though the options are more specialized than standard credit card categories. Since you are currently paying $2,350 monthly to Cascade Apartments—totaling $28,200 per year—finding a fee-free way to capture rewards could effectively "discount" your housing costs by several hundred dollars annually.
The bilt rewards ecosystem
The most effective way to earn back on rent in 2026 without a transaction fee is through the Bilt Rewards program. Based on current market offerings, their primary credit card allows you to pay rent via a linked ACH account or a physical check sent to your landlord, while they absorb the typical 3% processing fee.
Earning structure: The Bilt Blue card (no annual fee) typically earns 1x point per dollar on rent, up to 100,000 points per calendar year. For your $2,350 rent, that is 2,350 points every month.
The "active use" requirement: To earn any points during a statement cycle, you must use the card for at least 5 non-rent transactions.
Value of points: If you transfer these points to travel partners like Hyatt or major airlines, they are often valued at approximately 1.5 to 2.0 cents each. This means your monthly rent could yield roughly $35 to $47 in travel value for $0 in fees.
Strategic sign-up bonuses
While a 3% fee is usually a "bad deal" for standard cashback, it can be a smart move if you are trying to hit a large sign-up bonus on a new card. For instance, if a new card offers a $750 bonus after spending $4,000 in three months, the math shifts significantly in your favor.
The math: Using your $2,350 rent as a one-time payment through a portal with a 3% fee would cost you $70.50 in fees.
The benefit: That single payment covers more than half of your spending requirement. Even after the $70.50 fee, the $750 bonus leaves you with a net gain of $679.50.
When to stop: Once you have earned the bonus, you should immediately switch back to a fee-free method like ACH, as a 3% fee will almost always outweigh standard 1.5% or 2% cashback rates.
Cash float optimization
If you prefer not to open new credit lines, you can still "earn" on your rent by optimizing the timing of your payments using a high-yield savings account (HYSA), like your existing Ally account.
The strategy: Keep your rent money in your HYSA until the very last moment before the due date.
The yield: At a hypothetical 4.50% annual percentage yield, holding your $2,350 rent in savings for an extra 30 days generates approximately $8.80 in interest per month.
Total impact: While $8.80 feels small, it is a $105 annual "return" on your rent with zero risk and zero fees. Just ensure your transfer to your checking account is initiated 2–3 business days before the rent is due to avoid late fees.
Would you like me to help you compare the total annual value of these different reward strategies based on your current spending patterns? 📈
